claimText: Seneca Foods (SENEA) trades below tangible book value on a FIFO-adjusted basis.
latestFinancialContribution: Anchored FIFO-adjusted equity (after-tax 24.9%) at $902,823,000; reported equity at $633,023,000
disagreementNote: Linear track ranks mechanism bias positive; parallel track flags aggregator combined SENEA + SENEB market cap (~$1.8-1.9b) is above $902.8m FIFO-adjusted equity. Both positions preserved.

claimText: Seneca trades at or below NCAV when inventory is restated to FIFO.
latestFinancialContribution: Anchored FIFO-adjusted NCAV (after-tax 24.9%) at $477,048,000; reported NCAV at $207,248,000

claimText: Reported earnings materially understate FIFO earnings power because of a $359.3m LIFO reserve.
latestFinancialContribution: Anchored FY2025 LIFO charge at $34,474,000; FIFO EBITDA $171,432,000; adjusted net earnings $67,114,000; statutory tax rate 24.9%
disagreementNote: Both upstream tracks classify as partial. Latest-financial track materially advances the bridge by anchoring multiple numerical inputs. C-003a (LIFO reserve magnitude) supported across all three tracks; C-003b (FIFO earnings power across cycles) pending multi-year bridge (M-004).

claimText: Seneca has compounded book value per share over a 20-year window despite minimal sell-side coverage.
latestFinancialContribution: Out of scope for the latest-financial single-year rebuild

claimText: U.S. private-label vegetable canning is an oligopoly in which Seneca and Lakeside Foods account for ~90% of supply.
disagreementNote: No disagreement. Both tracks agree Lakeside primary material is not retrieved; Q-001 (which competitor Lakeside names) and Q-IS-A (national vs regional measurement basis) are blocking.

claimText: Del Monte Pacific deconsolidated its U.S. Del Monte Foods business effective May 2025; that subsidiary subsequently filed for Chapter 11.
disagreementNote: Parallel track sharpens linear classification by distinguishing terminal events (DMC-07, DMC-08 anchored to S-003 and S-008) from pre-2025 conduct sequence (DMC-01..DMC-06 with no source ID). Master adopts parallel framing.

claimText: SENEA was deleted from the S&P SmallCap 600 in July 2023, producing forced selling pressure independent of fundamentals.
parallelTrackConfidence: high (in unresolved verdict; data not collected)
disagreementNote: Linear grades partial because deletion is pointer-anchored; parallel grades unresolved because no event-window magnitude can be computed without OHLCV / ETF holdings / factor returns. Both positions preserved.

claimText: Seneca has executed material share repurchases relative to float, supporting per-share compounding.
disagreementNote: DISAGREEMENT. Parallel corroborates March 2021 modified Dutch auction (1,449,339 Class A at $51.62, ~$74.81m, ~17.07% of relevant float) via issuer press releases; linear track does not credit press-release-level evidence. Master adopts parallel framing for the single corroborated event; full ledger remains pending.

claimText: Seneca has minimal sell-side analyst coverage, contributing to the discount.
disagreementNote: Linear grades as partial at the source-pointer level (S-006); parallel grades as unresolved because S-006 has not been retrieved.

claimText: Seneca operates a dual-class share structure with concentrated insider/family voting control.
disagreementNote: Both tracks agree dual-class structure is anchored and combined family voting share is pending. Linear grades structure point as partial; parallel grades family-voting sub-claim as unresolved. Master synthesis: partial on structure; voting concentration unresolved.

claimText: Seneca has demonstrated pricing-cost recovery across crop, steel/tinplate, freight, and labor cycles.
latestFinancialContribution: Out of scope for single-year rebuild
disagreementNote: No substantive disagreement. Linear defers; parallel did not include this family. Master synthesis preserves as unresolved-deferred.
